simpleHelp: An environmental policy is a key governance tool which serves as a foundation to drive environmental action while ensuring accountability. This question informs data users about the organization's commitments regarding environmental issues by providing an insight into its level of awareness of those issues and demonstrating the robustness of actions outlined in the policy.

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes" in response to column 1 "Does your organization have any environmental policies?" of 4.6.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes" in response to 열 1 "Does 귀사 have 하나 이상 environmental policies?" of 4.6.

guidance.text:
General 
If you have no stand-alone environmental policy, provide details on your equivalent policy documents that include the directions and goals regarding your environmental performance.
If you have set targets related to any of the commitments selected in column 5 "Environmental policy content", you should report the target's details in the corresponding Targets questions in the ‘Environmental Performance' modules. For example, if you have a commitment to ‘No-conversion of natural ecosystems', you should provide further information on cutoff dates in the Targets question 8.7.1.
Level of coverage (column 2) 
Select "Organization-wide" if the level of coverage applies to your entire organization, e.g., if the policy is applied at the corporate level rather than a geographical or facility level. Otherwise, choose another option and provide more details and an explanation in column 4 "Explain the coverage".
Value chain stages covered (column 3) 
Indicate the stages of the value chain covered in the content of your policy, e.g., whether the content dropdowns selected in column 5 "Environmental policy content" extend to stages of the value chain beyond your direct operations.
This is different from column 2 "Level of coverage", as the former refers to the scope of the policy, while this column requests information on whether the contents of the policy, i.e. commitments, only address your direct operations or also other parts of your value chain. For example, an organization could have an organization-wide policy that only relates to their direct operations, and a country-specific environmental policy that extends to upstream value chain (i.e., their suppliers).
If some response options in column 5 "Environmental policy content" only apply to a particular value chain stage, add rows as applicable; e.g., if you have one organization-wide policy but different commitments apply to different stages of your value chain. 
Explain the coverage (column 4) 
Briefly describe the coverage of your environmental policy and provide reasons.
If applicable, specify which biomes or ecoregions are covered by your policy, e.g., Cerrado biome.
Indicate whether there are exclusions to the coverage of your policy e.g., geographical areas, business activities etc. If there are exclusions, provide the reasons.
Attach the policy (column 8) 
This column is optional if you select "Not publicly available" in column "Public availability".
guidance.textKo: 일반 만약 you have no stand-alone environmental 정책, 세부 정보를 입력 on your equivalent 정책 documents that include the directions 및 goals regarding your environmental performance. 만약 you have set 목표 related to 하나 이상 의 commitments 선택 in 열 5 "Environmental 정책 content", you 해야 합니다 report the 목표's details in the corresponding 목표 questions in the ‘Environmental Performance' modules. For example, 만약 you have a commitment to ‘No-conversion of natural ecosystems', you 해야 합니다 provide further information on cutoff dates in the 목표 문항 8.7.1. Level of coverage (열 2) Select "Organization-wide" 만약 the level of coverage applies to your entire organization, e.g., 만약 the 정책 is applied at the corporate level rather than a geographical 또는 facility level. Otherwise, choose another option 및 provide more details 및 an explanation in 열 4 "Explain the coverage". 가치사슬 stages covered (열 3) Indicate the stages 의 가치사슬 covered in the content of your 정책, e.g., whether the content dropdowns 선택 in 열 5 "Environmental 정책 content" extend to stages 의 가치사슬 beyond your 직접 운영사업장. This is different from 열 2 "Level of coverage", as the former refers to the scope 의 정책, while this 열 requests information on whether the contents 의 정책, i.e. commitments, only address your 직접 운영사업장 또는 also other parts of your 가치사슬. For example, an organization could have an organization-wide 정책 that only relates to their 직접 운영사업장, 및 a country-specific environmental 정책 that extends to 업스트림 가치사슬 (i.e., their suppliers). 만약 some response options in 열 5 "Environmental 정책 content" only apply to a particular 가치사슬 stage, add 행 as 해당되는 경우; e.g., 만약 you have one organization-wide 정책 but different commitments apply to different stages of your 가치사슬. Explain the coverage (열 4) Briefly 설명 the coverage of your environmental 정책 및 provide reasons. 만약 해당되는 경우, specify which biomes 또는 ecoregions are covered by your 정책, e.g., Cerrado biome. Indicate whether there are 제외사항 to the coverage of your 정책 e.g., geographical areas, business activities etc. 만약 there are 제외사항, provide the reasons. Attach the 정책 (열 8) This 열 is optional 만약 you select "Not publicly available" in 열 "Public availability".

guidance.text:
The organization has publicly available policies which address environmental dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities related to the organization and its value chain.
The policies apply organization-wide, and to the organization's value chain where relevant.
The organization endorses or aligns its environmental policies and commitments to the most recent and widely accepted scientific interpretation of relevant global environmental treaties, policy goals (e.g., the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, Paris Agreement, Sustainable Development Goals, Planetary Boundaries) and identifies where and how they align.
guidance.textKo: The organization has publicly available policies which address 환경 의존성, 임팩트, 리스크 및 기회 related to the organization 및 its 가치사슬. The policies apply organization-wide, 및 to the 조직의 가치사슬 여기서 relevant. The organization endorses 또는 aligns its environmental policies 및 commitments to the most recent 및 widely accepted scientific interpretation of relevant global environmental treaties, 정책 goals (e.g., the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, Paris Agreement, Sustainable Development Goals, Planetary Boundaries) 및 identifies 여기서 및 how they align.

guidance.text:
Commitment: a statement that specifies an environmental action that the organization intends to take regarding its management of or performance on environmental issues across its activities and business relationships. As good practice, commitments should be publicly available. Commitments are distinct from targets or operational procedures (e.g., sourcing codes, supplier requirements, and standard operating procedures) by which organizations may operationalize their commitments or sustainability initiatives (adapted from AFi, 2024 ). 
High Conservation Value : biological, ecological, social or cultural values which are considered outstandingly significant or critically important, at the national, regional or global level ( High Conservation Values (HCV) Resource Network, 2024 ). 
Landscape and jurisdictional initiative:  an on-the-ground collaborative program to set common goals, take collective action while reconciling different interests, and monitor progress towards improving social, environmental, and economic outcomes at a landscape/jurisdictional scale.
Threatened and protected species: all species assessed as a threatened category on IUCN's Red List of Threatened Species, including critically endangered, endangered, and vulnerable species, as well as other species considered threatened or protected under international, national, or subnational laws and regulations.
Zero‐carbon‐ready building : a building that is highly energy efficient based on local rating schemes and uses either renewable energy directly or an energy supply that will be fully decarbonized by 2050, such as electricity or district heat ( SBTi , adapted from IEA, 2025 ).
guidance.textKo: Commitment: a statement that specifies an environmental action that the organization intends to take regarding its management of 또는 performance on environmental issues across its activities 및 business relationships. As good practice, commitments 해야 합니다 be publicly available. Commitments are distinct from 목표 또는 operational 절차s (e.g., sourcing codes, supplier requirements, 및 standard operating 절차s) by which organizations may operationalize their commitments 또는 sustainability 이니셔티브 (adapted from AFi, 2024 ). High Conservation Value : biological, ecological, social 또는 cultural values which are considered outstandingly significant 또는 critically important, at the national, regional 또는 global level ( High Conservation Values (HCV) Resource Network, 2024 ). Landscape 및 jurisdictional 이니셔티브: an on-the-ground collaborative program to set common goals, take collective action while reconciling different interests, 및 monitor progress towards improving social, environmental, 및 economic outcomes at a landscape/jurisdictional scale. Threatened 및 protected species: all species assessed as a threatened category on IUCN's Red List of Threatened Species, 포함 critically endangered, endangered, 및 vulnerable species, as well as other species considered threatened 또는 protected under international, national, 또는 subnational laws 및 regulations. Zero‐carbon‐ready building : a building that is highly energy efficient based on local rating schemes 및 uses either 재생에너지 directly 또는 an energy supply that will be fully decarbonized by 2050, such as electricity 또는 district heat ( SBTi , adapted from IEA, 2025 ).

simpleHelpKo: An environmental 정책 is a key governance tool which serves as a foundation to drive environmental action while ensuring accountability. This 문항 informs 데이터 사용자 about the 조직의 commitments regarding environmental issues by providing an insight into its level of awareness of those issues 및 demonstrating the robustness of actions outlined in the 정책.
